rationale: Current title is Graphic Designer with a career spanning manufacturing operations, civil engineering, and project management at fictional/services companies (Wayne Enterprises, Pied Piper), zero ML/IR/retrieval experience, and only a beginner LangChain skill of 6 months indicating a very recent pivot with no engineering depth.

Full Stack Developer with 3.5 years at IT-services (Mphasis) and a fictional company (Initech), no production ML or retrieval experience, and AI exposure limited to online courses and LangChain tutorials — exactly the 'recent LangChain-to-OpenAI' profile the JD explicitly disqualifies.

Content Writer with 14 years in non-technical roles (content, mechanical engineering, accounting) split across Globex, Wipro IT services, and fictional Wayne Enterprises — no ML, NLP, retrieval, or engineering skills relevant to a Senior AI Engineer role.

Entire 8-year career is at TCS IT services in non-technical roles (Content Writer, Mechanical Engineer titles with mismatched descriptions suggesting fabricated data), with skills like Salesforce CRM and Tally — no ML, NLP, or retrieval engineering experience whatsoever.

Sales Executive / HR Manager background across Initech, Infosys, Hooli, and fictional Dunder Mifflin with skills limited to Go, Hadoop, MongoDB, and Excel — no ML or IR experience, and a current role at a known fictional company is a hard disqualifier.

Frontend Engineer with only 2.1 years of experience, single role at fictional Stark Industries doing Android/mobile development, and AI exposure limited to self-taught online courses and a small RAG side project — far below the production ML/retrieval depth the role requires.

Mechanical Engineer title with a career spanning consulting, customer support, and sales at Initech, Infosys, fictional Stark Industries, and fictional Dunder Mifflin — no technical ML or retrieval engineering background despite an M.S. in Machine Learning, and the summary describes a marketing manager profile.

Content Writer with 3.2 years entirely in SEO/content strategy at fictional Stark Industries, with no engineering or ML skills — beginner-level Spring Boot and GCP with under 17 months each represent keyword inflation with zero relevance to production AI/IR systems.

AI Research Engineer with 5.1 years including product-company experience at Flipkart (e-commerce) and PolicyBazaar, advanced skills in Qdrant (vector DB), Hugging Face Transformers, XGBoost, and LLMs with strong endorsements, plus an IIIT Bangalore M.S. — solid ML production background though the career descriptions suggest predictive modeling focus rather than explicit hybrid retrieval/ranking system ownership.

Cloud Engineer with 3.8 years in QA/test automation at a manufacturing company, with an implausibly advanced LoRA skill (60 endorsements, 53 months — longer than their entire career) suggesting keyword inflation, and no evidence of production retrieval, search, or ranking systems.
